trigger_pattern: age ≥ 65 AND all vitals normal AND vague complaint
message: Atypical risk: elderly, normal vitals, nonspecific
rationale: Silent MI/sepsis can present without vital derangement

trigger_pattern: two or more borderline-normal vitals together
rationale: Each passes alone; jointly suggests early deterioration

trigger_pattern: all vitals normal AND pain ≥ 7 AND high-risk complaint (chest/abdominal)
message: Severe pain, normal vitals - do not under-triage
rationale: Chest ~16% / abdominal ~15% admit despite normal vitals

trigger_pattern: diabetic AND (vague malaise OR altered behaviour)
rationale: Silent ischemia / DKA can mask as vague symptoms

trigger_pattern: any stroke-symptom keyword / FAST-positive (regardless of vitals)
rationale: Stroke complaints ~15% admit; treatment clock drives urgency
evidence_source: NHAMCS 2022 (n=846, 15.0%) + ESI guideline + AHA/ASA stroke guideline

trigger_pattern: (pregnant OR postpartum) AND headache AND systolic BP ≥ 140
rationale: Hypertensive disorders of pregnancy; leading maternal cause
evidence_source: ACOG Hypertension in Pregnancy (illustrative; small-n in NHAMCS)

trigger_pattern: postpartum AND (heavy bleeding OR breathlessness OR chest pain)
rationale: Postpartum "looks-fine-but-isn't" window; small-n in public data
Febrile neonate = automatic escalation for serious bacterial infection

trigger_pattern: heart rate elevated AND systolic BP low (any complaint)
immunocompromised (chemo OR transplant OR chronic steroids) AND temp ≥ 38°C
rationale: Fever in immunosuppression = neutropenic fever until excluded

trigger_pattern: sickle cell disease AND (fever OR chest symptoms)
rationale: Fever or chest signs in SCD = high-risk, time-critical; ACS is leading cause of SCD death
